notes: Recaptured after replacing the {1.8 grip / 2.8 drift} step cap on body-frame angular velocity with a speed-scheduled continuous cap (2.8 at ≤40 km/h → 1.5 at ≥220 km/h, half-cosine blend). Removes the perceptible 'snap' when ω lingered at the grip cap long enough to push slip past the drift threshold and the cap jumped by ~1 rad/s in a single frame. Dry lat-g drops from 9.94 to 8.12 because the prior peak was saturated against the legacy ±10G sanitize ceiling; the new value is closer to physically plausible high-downforce cornering. Low-grip lat-g rises slightly because oil/wet were previously cap-limited too. All ±20-25% drift bands remain green.
